description: Milestone 7 — True 3D: Megabonk-style mouse-orbit camera with camera-relative WASD, real jump physics with coyote time and jump buffering, deterministic heightmapped terrain shared between server simulation and client prediction, environmental props, and sky/atmosphere. Two manual BLOCKING gates: camera review after US-007, final friend playtest after US-018. Source PRD: tasks/prd-m7-verticality.md. Architectural rules in CLAUDE.md are binding — schema in shared/schema.ts, messages in shared/messages.ts, logic in shared/rules.ts, no Math.random in gameplay code, no name-based branching in tick/render code, tick order from rule 11 unchanged.

userStories.title: Shared deterministic terrain function
userStories.description: As the simulation, I need a single terrainHeight(x, z) function imported by both server and client so prediction and authoritative simulation produce identical Y positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add simplex-noise and alea as runtime deps in packages/shared/package.json (per CLAUDE.md exception for cross-runtime determinism)
Create packages/shared/src/terrain.ts exporting initTerrain(seed: number) and terrainHeight(x: number, z: number): number
Multi-octave noise: scale1=0.02 amp=4, scale2=0.08 amp=1.2, scale3=0.20 amp=0.3 (sum)
Spawn-area flattening: smoothly damp height to 0 within 8 units of origin using h * t * t ramp where t = distFromOrigin / 8
No hidden mutable state across calls — same (seed, x, z) returns the same float every time
Vitest: terrainHeight returns identical values for the same (seed, x, z) after re-init
Vitest: heights within 8 units of origin are within ±0.05 of zero
Vitest: snapshot test — 5 known (seed, x, z) → height tuples assert exact float equality (this is the early-warning system for prediction desync)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Server snaps player Y to terrain
userStories.description: As the server, I keep each player's Y attached to the terrain so the world has shape before any rendering work begins. No jump yet — vy is integrated to zero.
acceptanceCriteria: Add y: number, vy: number, grounded: boolean to Player schema in packages/shared/src/schema.ts (CLAUDE.md rule 2)
RoomState carries the seed (likely already present from CLAUDE.md rule 6) and GameRoom calls initTerrain(state.seed) on room creation
tickPlayers in shared/rules.ts sets player.y = terrainHeight(player.x, player.z) + PLAYER_GROUND_OFFSET each tick
PLAYER_GROUND_OFFSET added to shared/constants.ts (start at 0; revisit if mesh origin requires offset — see PRD Q2)
Tick order from CLAUDE.md rule 11 is unchanged
Vitest integration test: drive an input that walks a player across known terrain coordinates, assert player.y matches terrainHeight at each tick
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Client renders terrain mesh with slope shader
userStories.description: As a player, I see a real 3D world with hills and valleys instead of a flat void.
acceptanceCriteria: On room join, client calls initTerrain(state.seed) using the same seed as the server
PlaneGeometry(TERRAIN_SIZE, TERRAIN_SIZE, 200, 200) rotated -π/2 on X to be horizontal, vertices displaced by terrainHeight
geo.computeVertexNormals() called after vertex displacement
Custom slope-based ShaderMaterial: grass color (0.3, 0.55, 0.2) on flat areas, rock color (0.4, 0.35, 0.3) on steep, mixed via smoothstep(0.2, 0.5, 1 - vNormal.y)
Map boundary marker from M6 replaced with a tall cylinder (simplest visible-from-any-angle approach per brief)
60fps holds with terrain mesh in scene
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Visual / 60fps verification in browser DEFERRED — no dev-browser skill available in this environment. Reviewer should manually pnpm dev and walk the spawn area to confirm slope shader, displaced mesh, and tall-cylinder boundary.

userStories.title: Render players at correct Y
userStories.description: As a player, my character and other players sit on the terrain instead of floating at y=0.
acceptanceCriteria: Local-player mesh reads player.y from predicted state (still no jump — Y is purely terrain-derived for now)
Remote-player meshes read interpolated Y — extend the existing two-snapshot interpolation buffer to include Y (same lerp, same buffer, no new buffer)
Standing on a hill: cube sits flush with terrain mesh visually
Walking up/down hills: cube Y tracks smoothly without jitter
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Visual flush-with-terrain / no-jitter verification in browser DEFERRED — no dev-browser skill available in this environment. Reviewer should manually pnpm dev with two clients and walk both onto a visible hill to confirm cubes sit flush with the rendered terrain mesh and remote-player Y interpolates smoothly.

userStories.title: Mouse-orbit camera with pointer lock and click-to-play overlay
userStories.description: As a player, I drag the camera around the player with my mouse. The camera orbits, follows smoothly, and clicking the canvas captures the cursor.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/client/src/camera.ts with constants block: CAMERA_DISTANCE=9, CAMERA_LOOK_HEIGHT=1.2, CAMERA_PITCH_MIN=-10°, CAMERA_PITCH_MAX=60°, CAMERA_PITCH_DEFAULT=35°, MOUSE_SENSITIVITY_X=0.0025, MOUSE_SENSITIVITY_Y=0.0020, CAMERA_FOLLOW_LERP=18 (radians/seconds where appropriate)
Yaw direction convention documented in a comment in camera.ts (which way does the camera orbit when yaw increases)
Pointer lock: clicking the canvas requests pointer lock; pointerlockchange and pointerlockerror listeners maintain locked state; ESC and tab-out release
Click-to-play overlay component covers the canvas whenever pointer is not locked (including initial load, after ESC, after tab-out); clicking the overlay re-requests lock
Mouse handler (only while locked): yaw -= movementX * MOUSE_SENSITIVITY_X, pitch -= movementY * MOUSE_SENSITIVITY_Y, pitch clamped to [PITCH_MIN, PITCH_MAX], yaw unbounded

Camera position computed each frame from yaw/pitch orbit math: targetX = player.x + DIST*sin(yaw)*cos(pitch), targetY = player.y + DIST*sin(pitch) + LOOK_HEIGHT, targetZ = player.z + DIST*cos(yaw)*cos(pitch); lookAt = (player.x, player.y + LOOK_HEIGHT, player.z)
Camera follow uses frame-rate-independent lerp: t = 1 - exp(-CAMERA_FOLLOW_LERP * dt)
Old fixed-rotation camera from M6 disabled/removed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Visual verification in browser DEFERRED — no dev-browser skill available in this environment. Reviewer should manually pnpm dev to confirm pointer lock engages on canvas/overlay click, ESC releases, mouse drag orbits, and the camera follow feels smooth before flipping US-007 (manual gate).

userStories.title: Camera-relative WASD, facing-from-movement, remove crosshair
userStories.description: As a player, W moves me into the screen regardless of camera angle, and my character rotates to face my movement direction (not the camera).
acceptanceCriteria: WASD raw inputs collected as 2D vector in camera space (W=-Z, S=+Z, A=-X, D=+X), normalized for diagonals
Transform to world space using camera yaw ONLY (NOT pitch): worldX = inputX*cos(yaw) - inputZ*sin(yaw), worldZ = inputX*sin(yaw) + inputZ*cos(yaw)
world (X, Z) sent in the dir field of the existing input message
input message in packages/shared/src/messages.ts no longer carries a facing field (CLAUDE.md rule 3 — single discriminated union edited in one file)
Server derives player facing from movement direction inside tickPlayers (logic stays in shared/rules.ts per rule 4 — handlers stay thin)
Player visual mesh rotation lerps smoothly toward the movement direction; when stopped, last facing holds
Player does NOT rotate to face the camera
M6 crosshair removed entirely (no replacement reticle this milestone)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Visual verification in browser DEFERRED — no dev-browser skill available in this environment. Reviewer should manually pnpm dev to confirm W moves into the screen at any camera yaw, the player rotates to face movement (not the camera), the crosshair is gone, and stopped-player facing holds before flipping US-007.

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Camera review checkpoint (manual)
userStories.description: As the project owner, I play the camera before committing the next 8+ implementation steps to a foundation that may need to change. Camera feel is the highest tactical risk in the milestone.
acceptanceCriteria: MANUAL GATE — Ralph cannot auto-pass this story. A human (Luke) must play and approve, then flip passes=true manually.
Build runs locally without errors after US-001 through US-006
Luke has played for at least one session (~10 min) with the new mouse-orbit camera
Luke has explicitly approved the feel OR provided concrete tuning changes that have been applied and re-tested
If approved: final approved values committed back to camera.ts constants block (so they survive)
If not approved: iterate on MOUSE_SENSITIVITY_X/Y, CAMERA_PITCH_DEFAULT, CAMERA_FOLLOW_LERP, CAMERA_DISTANCE before US-008+ begins
No work on US-008 or beyond until this story is closed
userStories.notes: MANUAL CHECKPOINT — approved by Luke after playtest of US-001..US-006.

userStories.title: Camera terrain occlusion (raycast pull-in)
userStories.description: As a player, when I walk near a hill, the camera does not clip into terrain.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-frame Raycaster from (player.x, player.y + CAMERA_LOOK_HEIGHT, player.z) toward desired camera position
Ray length equals desired camera distance; near=0.1, far=ray length
intersectObject targets ONLY the terrain mesh (not props, players, enemies)
On hit: camera placed at hitDistance - 0.3 along ray direction, clamped to a minimum of 0.5 from player
On no-hit: camera goes to desired orbit position (US-005 behavior unchanged)
Walking in/out of a hill's shadow does not produce visible camera jitter
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Core jump physics — gravity, jump input, ground snap
userStories.description: As a player, pressing space makes me jump. Gravity pulls me down, and I snap to the terrain when I land.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to packages/shared/src/constants.ts: GRAVITY=25, JUMP_VELOCITY=9, TERMINAL_FALL_SPEED=30 (PLAYER_GROUND_OFFSET already added in US-002)
Extend input message in packages/shared/src/messages.ts to include jump: boolean (CLAUDE.md rule 3 — single file edit)
Client sets jump=true on the input tick where the player pressed space (key-down detection, single-tick true)
tickPlayers in shared/rules.ts: apply gravity to vy (player.vy -= GRAVITY * dt), clamp to -TERMINAL_FALL_SPEED, integrate y (player.y += player.vy * dt)
Ground check: groundY = terrainHeight(x, z) + PLAYER_GROUND_OFFSET; if player.y <= groundY then snap player.y = groundY, set vy = 0, set grounded = true; else grounded = false
Jump (this story, no forgiveness yet): if input.jump and player.grounded then player.vy = JUMP_VELOCITY, grounded = false
No Math.random introduced (CLAUDE.md rule 6); jump fully deterministic from inputs
Vitest: peak jump height ≈ JUMP_VELOCITY² / (2 * GRAVITY) within 1%
Vitest: ground snap — player at y < terrain → snapped to terrain, vy=0, grounded=true
Server integration test: player joins, sends jump input, full Y trajectory matches expected within tick-by-tick assertions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Jump forgiveness — coyote time and jump buffer
userStories.description: As a player, my jump is forgiving — I can still jump shortly after running off a ledge, and pressing jump just before landing executes the jump on landing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to shared/constants.ts: COYOTE_TIME=0.1, JUMP_BUFFER=0.1 (seconds)
Add to Player schema: lastGroundedAt: number (tick), jumpBufferedAt: number (tick, -1 = none) (CLAUDE.md rule 2)
tickPlayers updates lastGroundedAt = state.tick whenever grounded becomes/stays true
Helper canJump(player, tick): true if player.grounded OR ((tick - player.lastGroundedAt) * (1/TICK_RATE)) <= COYOTE_TIME
Buffered jump: if input.jump arrives while NOT canJump, set player.jumpBufferedAt = state.tick
On any subsequent tick where canJump becomes true AND (state.tick - jumpBufferedAt) * (1/TICK_RATE) <= JUMP_BUFFER: execute jump (vy = JUMP_VELOCITY, grounded = false), clear jumpBufferedAt = -1
Vitest: walk off ledge then jump after 0.05s — succeeds; jump after 0.15s — fails
Vitest: press jump 0.05s before landing — jump executes on landing tick
Tick order from CLAUDE.md rule 11 unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Client prediction extends to Y / vy / grounded
userStories.description: As a player, when I jump my local character peaks and lands smoothly without rubber-banding from server reconciliation.
acceptanceCriteria: Client-side predicted Player state includes y, vy, grounded, lastGroundedAt, jumpBufferedAt

Reconciliation: on each server snapshot, re-apply unacknowledged inputs (seq > Player.lastProcessedInput) on top of the server's authoritative (x, y, z, vy, grounded, lastGroundedAt, jumpBufferedAt) to produce the predicted current frame (CLAUDE.md rule 9)
Replay imports terrainHeight from packages/shared (same function as server) and the same physics constants from shared/constants.ts
Visual: jump on flat ground, on a hill, while running — no rubber-band, no jitter at landing
Two-client determinism check: open two clients, both jump at the same tick, observed Y traces match within snapshot-interpolation tolerance
No extrapolation introduced (CLAUDE.md "Things NOT to do")
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Enemies snap to terrain
userStories.description: As an enemy, I walk on the ground and follow hills/valleys instead of floating.
acceptanceCriteria: Add y: number to Enemy schema in packages/shared/src/schema.ts (CLAUDE.md rule 2)
Add ENEMY_GROUND_OFFSET to shared/constants.ts
tickEnemies in shared/rules.ts: after movement integration, enemy.y = terrainHeight(enemy.x, enemy.z) + ENEMY_GROUND_OFFSET
Enemies do NOT have vy and do NOT jump
Tick order from CLAUDE.md rule 11 unchanged
Client InstancedMesh for enemies: per-instance Y comes from interpolated SnapshotBuffer keyed by Enemy.id (CLAUDE.md rule 10 — never iteration order)
Visual: enemies walk smoothly up and down hills toward the player
If steep cliffs cause warping, the fix is reducing terrain noise amplitude in US-017 — NOT pathfinding (per brief)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Projectiles in 3D
userStories.description: As a player, I can jump over enemy projectiles, and my projectiles fire from my actual 3D position toward enemies' actual 3D positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Bolt projectiles: origin uses player Y, target uses enemy Y, motion is straight-line in 3D — no arc, no projectile-gravity
Orbit projectiles: orbit at player.y (not at y=0)
Hit detection uses 3D distance (Math.hypot(dx, dy, dz) against weapon radius)
Enemy projectiles (if Shooters from M6 exist): fire from enemy.y toward player position in 3D
Projectile-behavior weapons remain a closed-form function of the fire event payload on clients (CLAUDE.md rule 12) — fire payload now includes Y for both endpoints
Orbit-behavior weapons remain a closed-form function of (state.tick, player position incl. Y, weapon level) (CLAUDE.md rule 12)
damage_dealt and hit events include Y in their position payload so floating damage numbers spawn at the right altitude
No name-based branching added to tickWeapons, tickProjectiles, or render code (CLAUDE.md rule 12)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Generate environmental props deterministically (shared)
userStories.description: As the simulation, I provide a deterministic prop list both clients can render identically without any network sync.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/shared/src/props.ts exporting interface Prop { kind: number; x: number; z: number; y: number; rotation: number; scale: number } and generateProps(seed: number): Prop[]

Algorithm: alea PRNG seeded from seed.toString() + '_props' (separate sub-seed so prop randomness is independent of room rng), grid spacing 6, skip-radius 12 around origin, 60% prop probability per cell
Per-cell jitter ≤ 80% of spacing in both X and Z
y = terrainHeight(x, z) for each prop
Kind distribution: 50% trees (kind=0), 35% rocks (kind=1), 15% bushes (kind=2)
rotation random in [0, 2π); scale random in [0.8, 1.2)
Props are NOT in the schema (CLAUDE.md rule 2 derivation: regenerated on clients from state.seed)
Vitest: same seed → identical Prop[] (snapshot test on the first 20 props from a fixed seed)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Render props as InstancedMesh per kind (Quaternius GLTF)
userStories.description: As a player, I see trees, rocks, and bushes scattered across the terrain instead of empty space.
acceptanceCriteria: Source Quaternius CC0 GLTF assets for tree, rock, and bush families and place them in packages/client/public/assets/props/ (or equivalent)
Load each asset once via GLTFLoader on scene init
Render each kind as a single InstancedMesh with capacity sized for the prop count from generateProps(state.seed)
Per-instance position from the Prop entries; per-instance rotation around Y from prop.rotation; per-instance scale from prop.scale
Players walk through props — no collision (deferred per brief)
Two-client determinism check: both clients render props in identical positions
60fps holds with terrain + 200 enemies + props in scene
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Sky color, fog, and atmospheric lighting
userStories.description: As a player, the world looks like an outdoor place — distant terrain fades into sky and a sun illuminates the scene.
acceptanceCriteria: scene.background set to sky-blue color (start at 0x87ceeb, tunable in US-017)
scene.fog = new Fog(skyColor, 30, MAP_RADIUS * 1.5) so distant terrain fades to sky color rather than ending at a hard edge
DirectionalLight intensity ~0.9 positioned high (sun)
HemisphereLight(skyColor, 0x404020, 0.5) for fill
Shadows DEFERRED (per brief — too easy to misconfigure; defer to a future polish pass)
Visual: from any spot the horizon fades cleanly into sky rather than ending at a hard edge
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Polish-pass tuning (manual)
userStories.description: As the project owner, I tune the constants based on feel — the defaults from this milestone are starting points, not end states.
acceptanceCriteria: MANUAL — Ralph cannot auto-pass tuning-by-feel. A human (Luke) must play and decide. Flip passes=true manually.
Mouse sensitivity tuned (not nauseating, not dead) — final values written to camera.ts
Camera pitch default tuned for the new terrain (hills can change ideal pitch) — final value in camera.ts
Jump velocity vs gravity tuned for desired air time and peak — final values in shared/constants.ts; before/after values noted in commit message
Camera follow lerp tuned (not glued, not disconnected) — final value in camera.ts
Terrain amplitude tuned — if enemies warp on cliffs, REDUCE noise amplitude in shared/terrain.ts (NOT add pathfinding, per brief)
All final tuning values live in constants files (camera.ts, constants.ts, terrain.ts) — no magic numbers in component code
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: MANUAL — pause Ralph. Tuning is judgment-based and cannot be auto-passed. Mark passes=true after human iteration.

userStories.title: 🛑 BLOCKING — Final friend playtest (manual)
userStories.description: As the project owner, I playtest this milestone with the same friends from M5/M6 before declaring it complete. The whole milestone's goal is feel — that needs feedback, not silent assertion.
acceptanceCriteria: MANUAL GATE — Ralph cannot auto-pass this story. Human (Luke) must run the playtest and flip passes=true.
Build deployed somewhere the same friend group can join
At least one playtest session held (Discord + 5 players target)
Three feel questions asked explicitly: "Does the camera feel good? Is the jump satisfying? Does the world feel like a place?"
If any answer is "no" or "I guess so": at least one tuning iteration happens (back to US-017) before this story is closed — do not accept "it's fine I guess" per brief
Memory updated with any surprising playtest impressions as a new feedback memory
Milestone declared complete only after this story passes
userStories.notes: MANUAL CHECKPOINT — final gate for the milestone. Pause Ralph. Human review and friend-playtest required. Do NOT spawn iterations to try to pass this story automatically.
